   226→- `capabilities` field is declared but never read at dispatch sites — all dispatch guards use optional
   227→  chaining on the method itself (`agent?.auth`), not `agent.capabilities.auth`. The `capabilities`
   228→  object currently serves documentation purposes only (no runtime enforcement).
   229→- `updatePermissionMode` dispatch in `index.ts` silently no-ops when method is absent (no rejection),
   230→  while `auth` and `initWorkspace` reject with an error. Intentionally asymmetric: permission mode
   231→  is fire-and-forget; auth/workspaceInit are request-response.
   232→- Old method name stale references remain in: `claude-session.ts` JSDoc comments (lines 17, 49) and
   233→  three `console.log` strings in `claude-handler.ts` (lines 100, 119, 179). Not runtime bugs.
   234→- Test describe blocks in `claude-handler.test.ts` still use old names: `handleClaudeQuery` (line 144),
   235→  `handleClaudeCancel` (line 768), `handleClaudeUpdatePermissionMode` (line 798).
   236→
   237→## HTTP vs WS Query Parity Gap (PR "Create Workspace from PR or Branch")
   238→
   239→- `GET /workspaces/by-repo` HTTP fallback and query-engine `runQuery("workspaces")` both build `RepoGroup[]` independently.
   240→  When adding a field to one, must add to both. Confirmed: `git_origin_url` added to query-engine but NOT to HTTP route group object.
   241→- Full details in `http-vs-ws-parity.md`
   242→
   243→## createWorkspace Command Return Value (Pre-Existing Bug, #confirmed)
   244→
   245→- `WorkspaceService.create()` returns `as unknown as Workspace` — but the WS ack only has `{ accepted, commandId }`.
   246→- `workspace.id` on the result is `undefined`. `selectWorkspace(undefined)` no-ops silently.
   247→- Pre-existing since bad96ad4 (protocol unification). Does not cause visible breakage because WS subscription pushes snapshot.
   248→- Full details in `create-workspace-command-return.md`
   249→
   250→## Connection State Machine (features/connection)
   251→
   252→- Zustand store in `connectionStore.ts`: CONNECTED → GRACE_PERIOD (2s) → RECONNECTING (30s total) → DISCONNECTED
   253→- `onDisconnected` guard checks `current !== "connected"` — must allow re-entry from `disconnected`
   254→  to handle Retry button flow (forceReconnect fires notifyConnectionChange(false) before new socket opens)
   255→- `forceReconnect()` in platform/ws immediately calls `notifyConnectionChange(false)` — if guard blocks,
   256→  the banner freezes at DISCONNECTED with no recovery until socket connects
   257→- `emitSendAttemptFailed` in `connectionEvents.ts` is a module-level Set-based event bus (no React deps)
   258→- WS error messages that trigger `emitSendAttemptFailed`: both `"not connected"` AND `"disconnected"`
   259→  must be matched — `"WebSocket disconnected"` (from onclose pending-command rejection) will be missed
   260→  if only checking `"not connected"`
   261→- `platform/ws → connectionStore` is safe (no circular import). `session.queries → connectionEvents` is safe.
   262→- `ConnectionBanner` animates `height` (violates project guidelines) — causes panel jitter on 32→44 change
   263→- `ConnectionOrb` color crossfade: CSS `transition-colors` on a class swap is better than Framer Motion
   264→  `backgroundColor` interpolation (avoids paint-per-frame)
   265→
   266→## Mobile Web Layout Patterns
   267→
   268→- Full details in `mobile-layout-patterns.md`
   269→- `MobileLayout` has `key={selectedWorkspace.id}` at call site in MainContent.tsx — remounts on workspace switch, resetting `activeTab` state
   270→- `MobileTab` type defined once in MobileTabBar.tsx, imported in MobileLayout.tsx
   271→- `AllFilesDiffViewer` in mobile code view uses `hideHeader` prop to suppress close button
   272→- `MobileTabBar` safe-area-inset: uses `pb-[env(...)]` which compresses content; needs `min-h` adjustment
   273→- `MobileTabBar` has ARIA semantics: `role="tablist"`, `role="tab"`, `aria-selected`, `aria-controls`, `aria-labelledby`
   274→
   275→## parseContent Type Contract (Confirmed)
   276→
   277→- `parseContent` from `useSessionWithMessages` has type `(content: string) => (ContentBlock | string)[] | string`
   278→  — NOT `ContentBlock[]`. Returns `string` for plain-text user messages and envelope-format cancelled messages.
   279→- Any utility that accepts `parseContent` as a parameter MUST declare the full return type and guard
   280→  with `Array.isArray` before iterating. Declaring a narrower type (`ContentBlock[]`) compiles but
   281→  causes silent data loss at runtime (string is iterated character-by-character, all `isToolUseBlock`
   282→  guards return false).
   283→- Standard guard pattern: `const result = parseContent(msg.content); if (!Array.isArray(result)) continue;`
   284→
   285→## Reverse-Traverse + Flip Pattern for Message Extraction
   286→
   287→- Walking messages in reverse (newest→oldest) and collecting chunks, then calling `.reverse()` to
   288→  restore chronological order is correct at the message level.
   289→- When also walking blocks within each message in reverse, intra-message block order is ALSO reversed
   290→  before the final flip — resulting in wrong per-message block order after `.reverse()`.
   291→- Correct pattern: walk messages in reverse, but walk BLOCKS within each message FORWARD. Either
   292→  `unshift` message-level results to the output array, or accumulate per-message and prepend.
   293→
   294→## TOOL_COLORS Map Completeness
   295→
   296→- Every tool renderer registered in `registerTools.ts` should have a corresponding entry in `TOOL_COLORS`
   297→  in `toolColors.ts`. Missing entries don't cause crashes but leave other components that derive color
   298→  by tool name with `undefined` (falls through to no class).
   299→- When adding a new renderer, add its color entry to `TOOL_COLORS` in the same commit.
   300→## See Also
   301→
   302→- `patterns-deep.md` — overflow notes: error classification, chat virtualization, PRStatus, border radius, sidecar resume
   303→- `message-envelope-pattern.md` — session message envelope/flat array patterns
   304→

1→# Deep Pattern Notes (overflow from MEMORY.md)
     2→
     3→## Status Event Pipeline (Confirmed, 2026-03-04)
     4→
     5→- **Bug 1 (workspace matching)**: `session:status-changed` carries `workspaceId` (from `lookupWorkspaceId()` in session-writer).
     6→  Frontend matching: `(workspaceId && ws.id === workspaceId) || ws.current_session_id === sessionId`.
     7→  Fallback is backward-compat for Codex sessions and older events without workspaceId.
     8→- **Hardcoded `agentType: "claude"`** in `session-writer.ts` `sendStatusChanged()` calls — correct for now
     9→  (session-writer is only called from Claude path), but breaks if Codex sessions ever route through it.
    10→- **Bug 2 (Tauri null deserialization)**: `dbGetMessages()` in `src/platform/tauri/db.ts` now builds payload

input.new_string:
## Unread Session Indicator Pattern (Confirmed)

- Store: `useUnreadStore` in `features/session/store/unreadStore.ts` — Zustand + persist, `unreadSessionIds: Record<string, true>`.
- Mark unread: `useGlobalSessionNotifications` fires on `working → idle|error|needs_response|needs_plan_response`
  transition ONLY when `!isUserViewingSession(ws.id, sessionId)`.
- Mark read: two sites — `handleWorkspaceClick` in MainLayout (sidebar click), and `ChatArea` effect on `activeSessionId` change.
- **Design gap**: `sortByStatusPriority`, `getStatusCounts`, `getRepoPriorityStatus`, `groupByStatus` all call
  `getDisplayStatus(workspace)` WITHOUT `hasUnseenActivity`. Sidebar sorting and collapsed-repo badge color
  never reflect the unread store. Only `WorkspaceItem` renders the correct status.
- **Hook-after-conditional-return bug**: `useUnreadStore` is called at line 112 of `WorkspaceItem.tsx`, AFTER
  the `if (isInitializing) return` early exit at line 52. This violates React Rules of Hooks. The comment
  at line 45 ("Hooks must be called unconditionally") refers only to `useWorkingDuration` — the dev who added
  `useUnreadStore` missed the existing early return.
- **isUserViewingSession fallback**: `!layout.activeChatTabSessionId` means the session is assumed visible
  when no tab session is persisted (new workspace, first session). This is intentional and correct.
- **localStorage leak**: persisted unread state is never pruned for deleted/archived workspaces.
  Benign (orphan keys, ~10 bytes each), but accumulates over time.
- **`unreadSessionIds` full-map subscription in ChatArea**: `useUnreadStore((s) => s.unreadSessionIds)`
  subscribes to the entire map. Any workspace marking unread re-renders ChatArea. Should filter by
  `chatSessionIds` using a stable derived selector to avoid cross-workspace re-renders.

## See Also

- `patterns-deep.md` — overflow notes: error classification, chat virtualization, PRStatus, border radius, sidecar resume
- `message-envelope-pattern.md` — session message envelope/flat array patterns

content.content: The file /Users/zvada/Developer/agent/box-ide/.conductor/auckland-v5/.claude/agent-memory/code-reviewer/MEMORY.md has been updated successfully.

message.content: But if we close the app and open it again, this would be lost, right?

content.text:
No, it persists. The `unreadStore` uses Zustand's `persist` middleware with localStorage:

```ts
persist(
  (set, get) => ({ ... }),
  {
    name: "unread-sessions-store",
    version: 1,
  }
)
```

So unread indicators survive app restarts and page refreshes. The only thing that could cause a false "read" on cold start is if the session was already in `working` state before the app launched — since we detect the `working → idle` *transition*, a session that finished while the app was closed won't be marked unread. But that's covered by the OS-level notifications (`Glass` sound for "Agent finished") which already fire in that scenario.

The one gap: sessions from deleted/archived workspaces will accumulate as orphaned keys in localStorage over time. It's harmless (36 bytes per key) but if you want, I can add a `pruneStale` action that cleans up IDs no longer present in the workspace query cache. Want me to add that?

**Comment #3 (MainLayout.tsx):** You're saying: clicking a workspace shouldn't mark ALL sessions as read — only the one the user actually sees (the active tab). The other tabs should keep their unread dots until the user actually switches to them. That makes sense.

Let me fix all three.

Clean. Here's what changed for each comment:

**Comment #1 & #2 (ChatArea.tsx):** Removed the `useEffect` and the string-key serialization hack entirely. Now it's just a simple `useMemo` over the store map + a `handleTabChangeWithRead` callback wrapper that calls `markRead` inline when the user clicks a tab. Much simpler.

content.text:
Here is a compact summary of the most actionable findings, ordered by impact:

---

**The biggest issues, in order:**

1. **`RepositoryItem.tsx:55-69` — string-key hack again.** Same pattern you already caught in ChatArea. `repoSessionIds` memo → string join selector → second `useMemo` to split back to `Set`. Replace the whole block with one `useShallow` selector (~4 lines, ~18 deleted).

2. **`status.ts` — 5 dead exported functions (~70 lines).** `getUnreadCount`, `groupByStatus`, `getStatusCounts`, `getRepoUnreadCount`, `getRepoPriorityStatus` are all exported and never imported anywhere. Delete them.

3. **`unreadActions` in `unreadStore.ts:79-83` — pure pass-through with no value.** Every entry is `useUnreadStore.getState().X(id)`. That pattern already exists in this codebase for other stores and callers just call `.getState()` directly. The `isUnread` entry is never called from `unreadActions` at all. Delete the object, update 4 call sites.

4. **`ChatArea.tsx:67` — full-map subscription.** `useUnreadStore((s) => s.unreadSessionIds)` re-renders ChatArea whenever any session anywhere goes unread. Same `useShallow` fix as #1 — filter to `chatSessionIds` in the selector, drop the `useMemo`.

5. **`MainLayout.tsx:114-117` — `useEffect` to sync a ref.** The effect just does `ref.current = selectedWorkspaceId`. This is a plain render-body assignment; the effect adds an unnecessary microtask boundary.

6. **`isUserViewingSession` naming** — looks like a hook (camelCase predicate sitting above a hook export), but reads synchronously from `.getState()`. Rename to `checkIsViewingSession` or `getIsViewingSession`.

1→/**

     6→ *
     7→ * This is purely frontend state — no DB column needed. The store is
     8→ * persisted to localStorage so unread indicators survive page refreshes.

    17→
    18→import { create } from "zustand";
    19→import { devtools, persist } from "zustand/middleware";
    20→
    21→interface UnreadStore {
    22→  /** Set of session IDs with unseen activity */
    23→  unreadSessionIds: Record<string, true>;
    24→
    25→  /** Mark a session as having unseen activity */
    26→  markUnread: (sessionId: string) => void;

   124→ * Priority logic:
   125→ * 1. Error — session in error state
   126→ * 2. Unread — agent needs user response OR has unseen activity
   127→ * 3. Working — agent actively processing
   128→ * 4. Idle — dormant
   129→ *
   130→ * @param workspace Workspace data with session info
   131→ * @param hasUnseenActivity Whether the session has activity the user hasn't viewed yet
   132→ * @returns Derived display status for UI rendering
   133→ */
   134→export function getDisplayStatus(workspace: Workspace, hasUnseenActivity = false): DisplayStatus {
   135→  const status = workspace.session_status;
   136→
   137→  if (status === "error") return "error";
   138→  if (status === "needs_response" || status === "needs_plan_response") return "unread";
   139→  if (status === "working") return "working";
   140→  if (hasUnseenActivity) return "unread";
   141→  return "idle";
   142→}
   143→
   144→/**
   145→ * Sort workspaces by status priority
   146→ * Higher priority statuses appear first
   147→ *
   148→ * @param workspaces Array of workspaces to sort
   149→ * @param unreadSessionIds Optional set of session IDs with unseen activity
   150→ * @returns Sorted array (original array not mutated)
   151→ */
   152→export function sortByStatusPriority<T extends Workspace>(
   153→  workspaces: T[],
   154→  unreadSessionIds?: Set<string>
   155→): T[] {
   156→  return [...workspaces].sort((a, b) => {
   157→    const hasUnreadA = !!(a.current_session_id && unreadSessionIds?.has(a.current_session_id));
   158→    const hasUnreadB = !!(b.current_session_id && unreadSessionIds?.has(b.current_session_id));
   159→    const statusA = getDisplayStatus(a, hasUnreadA);
   160→    const statusB = getDisplayStatus(b, hasUnreadB);
   161→    const priorityA = STATUS_CONFIG[statusA].priority;
   162→    const priorityB = STATUS_CONFIG[statusB].priority;
   163→
   164→    // Higher priority first
   165→    if (priorityA !== priorityB) {
   166→      return priorityB - priorityA;
   167→    }
   168→
   169→    // Same priority: sort by latest activity (most recent first)
   170→    const timeA = new Date(a.updated_at).getTime();
   171→    const timeB = new Date(b.updated_at).getTime();
   172→    return timeB - timeA;
   173→  });
   174→}
   175→
   176→

content.content:
1→/**
     2→ * Unread Session Store
     3→ *
     4→ * Tracks which sessions have unseen activity (agent finished working,
     5→ * errored, or needs response while user wasn't viewing that session).
     6→ *
     7→ * This is purely frontend state — no DB column needed. The store is
     8→ * persisted to localStorage so unread indicators survive page refreshes.
     9→ *
    10→ * Mark as unread: when session transitions from "working" to any terminal
    11→ * state (idle, error, needs_response, needs_plan_response) and the user
    12→ * is not currently viewing that session.
    13→ *
    14→ * Mark as read: when user clicks on a workspace or switches to a tab
    15→ * containing the session.
    16→ */
    17→
    18→import { create } from "zustand";
    19→import { devtools, persist } from "zustand/middleware";
    20→
    21→interface UnreadStore {
    22→  /** Set of session IDs with unseen activity */
    23→  unreadSessionIds: Record<string, true>;
    24→
    25→  /** Mark a session as having unseen activity */
    26→  markUnread: (sessionId: string) => void;
    27→
    28→  /** Mark a session as read (user viewed it) */
    29→  markRead: (sessionId: string) => void;
    30→
    31→  /** Check if a session has unseen activity */
    32→  isUnread: (sessionId: string) => boolean;
    33→}
    34→
    35→export const useUnreadStore = create<UnreadStore>()(
    36→  devtools(
    37→    persist(
    38→      (set, get) => ({
    39→        unreadSessionIds: {},
    40→
    41→        markUnread: (sessionId) =>
    42→          set(
    43→            (state) => ({
    44→              unreadSessionIds: { ...state.unreadSessionIds, [sessionId]: true as const },
    45→            }),
    46→            false,
    47→            "unread/markUnread"
    48→          ),
    49→
    50→        markRead: (sessionId) =>
    51→          set(
    52→            (state) => {
    53→              if (!state.unreadSessionIds[sessionId]) return state;
    54→              const { [sessionId]: _, ...rest } = state.unreadSessionIds;
    55→              return { unreadSessionIds: rest };
    56→            },
    57→            false,
    58→            "unread/markRead"
    59→          ),
    60→
    61→        isUnread: (sessionId) => !!get().unreadSessionIds[sessionId],
    62→      }),
    63→      {
    64→        name: "unread-sessions-store",
    65→        version: 1,
    66→      }
    67→    ),
    68→    {
    69→      name: "unread-sessions-store",
    70→      enabled: import.meta.env.DEV,
    71→    }
    72→  )
    73→);
    74→
    75→/**
    76→ * Stable actions — call from anywhere without causing re-renders.
    77→ * Use these in effects, event handlers, and non-React code.
    78→ */
    79→export const unreadActions = {
    80→  markUnread: (sessionId: string) => useUnreadStore.getState().markUnread(sessionId),
    81→  markRead: (sessionId: string) => useUnreadStore.getState().markRead(sessionId),
    82→  isUnread: (sessionId: string) => useUnreadStore.getState().isUnread(sessionId),
    83→};
    84→

1→# Code Reviewer Memory
     2→
     3→## Key Architecture Patterns (Confirmed)
     4→
     5→- `react-resizable-panels` uses percentage values for `collapsedSize`/`minSize` — pixel↔percent
     6→  conversion via `panelGroupContainerRef` (excludes sidecar strip from container width math)
     7→- `workspaceLayoutStore` uses `version: 9` migrations (v9 added terminal tab state) — increment version when adding persisted fields
     8→- `cn()` uses `twMerge` internally — arbitrary `animate-[...]` classes conflict-resolve correctly
     9→  (last wins), so conditional breathing override pattern with `cn()` works as intended
    10→- `data-slot="resizable-panel"` is the CSS hook for the flex-grow transition in global.css
    11→
    12→## Animation Patterns
    13→
    14→- `strip-breathe` + `strip-settle` keyframes live in `global.css` (multi-component reuse)
    15→- Single-component animations should use Framer Motion inline, NOT global.css keyframes
    16→- `LazyMotion` with `domAnimation` wraps the whole app in `ThemeProvider.tsx` — always use `m`
    17→  (compact alias) instead of `motion`. `AgentQuestionOverlay.tsx` + `PlanApprovalOverlay.tsx` use
    18→  `motion` (pre-existing violation).
    19→
    20→## Dead Code Pattern (Confirmed)
    21→
    22→- When removing a prop from a call site, also check the destructuring site of the hook.
    23→  TypeScript does not error on unused destructured variables — only ESLint's `no-unused-vars` catches this.
    24→
    25→## CSS Architecture
    26→
    27→- `transition: flex-grow` on `[data-slot="resizable-panel"]` — flex-grow IS animatable per CSS spec
    28→- `window-resizing` class disables ALL layout transitions during native window resize
    29→- `[data-resize-handle-active]` sibling/parent selector disables panel transitions during drag
    30→
    31→## Cross-Component Event Bus Pattern
    32→
    33→- `window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("insert-to-chat", { detail }))` is the established
    34→  pattern for browser panel → chat input communication.
    35→
    36→## XML Attribute Serialization Risk Pattern
    37→
    38→- `serializeInspectElement` in `parseInspectTags.ts` embeds user-controlled strings (innerText,
    39→  path, tagName, reactComponent) into XML attributes with NO escaping. A `"` breaks parsing.
    40→  Fix: HTML-escape values before embedding.
    41→
    42→## Distribution / CI Patterns
    43→
    44→- `sed -i ''` is macOS/BSD syntax — fails on ubuntu-latest (GNU sed needs `sed -i "..."`).
    45→- Tauri updater `pubkey` empty string `""` disables signature verification entirely (security regression).
    46→- `minimumSystemVersion` for arm64-only builds should be `"11.0"` (Apple Silicon ships with 11.0).
    47→- The app spawns system `node` binary — hardened runtime notarization may need
    48→  `com.apple.security.cs.disable-library-validation` in Entitlements.plist.
    49→
    50→## Sentry / Observability Patterns (Confirmed)
    51→
    52→- **DSN propagation**: Rust `option_env!("SENTRY_DSN_RUST")` bakes frontend DSN at compile time.
    53→  `option_env!("SENTRY_DSN_NODE")` is baked into the binary and forwarded as `SENTRY_DSN` env var
    54→  to child processes (backend + sidecar). Frontend uses `import.meta.env.VITE_SENTRY_DSN`.
    55→- **`option_env!` with empty string**: When `SENTRY_DSN_RUST` is unset, `option_env!` returns `None`,
    56→  and `.unwrap_or("")` passes `""` to `sentry::init`. The sentry-rust crate treats empty DSN as
    57→  disabled — no panic, no-op client. This pattern is correct and safe.
    58→- **Conditional init in Node.js**: `if (process.env.SENTRY_DSN) { Sentry.init(...) }` means when
    59→  DSN is absent, Sentry is never initialized. `Sentry.captureException()` and `Sentry.close()` called
    60→  on an uninitialized client are safe no-ops in `@sentry/node@10`. Correct pattern.
    61→- **Frontend `enabled: !import.meta.env.DEV`**: Sentry is initialized but disabled in dev mode.
    62→  `captureException()` calls in `errorReporting.ts` are no-ops in dev mode as a result. Good behavior.
    63→- **`sendDefaultPii: true`** is set on all layers — intentional (desktop app, known users).
    64→- **Sentry guard lifetime (Rust)**: `_sentry_guard` in `main()` lives until process exit. Dropping
    65→  it earlier would flush pending events prematurely. The single-underscore prefix suppresses the
    66→  unused-variable warning without dropping it.
    67→- **`Sentry.close(2000)` before `process.exit`**: The 2-second flush window is appropriate. Both
    68→  backend and sidecar implement this correctly in their `uncaughtException` handlers.
    69→- **Hardcoded Sentry org/project in vite.config.ts**: `org: "deus-40"` and
    70→  `project: "deus-desktop-frontend"` are hardcoded. These are metadata identifiers (not secrets).
    71→  Leaking them poses minimal risk but couples the open-source repo to internal Sentry project names.
    72→- **CI missing Sentry secrets**: `release.yml` does not pass `SENTRY_DSN_RUST`, `SENTRY_DSN_NODE`,
    73→  `VITE_SENTRY_DSN`, or `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN`. Production builds will have Sentry monitoring disabled
    74→  until these are wired into the workflow.
    75→- **Root ErrorBoundary not connected to Sentry**: The outer `<ConditionalErrorBoundary fallback={DashboardError}>`
    76→  at line 126 of App.tsx has NO `onError` prop. React crashes inside MainLayout that are caught by
    77→  that boundary go unreported to Sentry. Only the inner boundary (line 187) calls `reportError`.
    78→- **`@sentry/node` in production dependencies**: `@sentry/node` and `@sentry/vite-plugin` are in
    79→  `dependencies` (not `devDependencies`). `@sentry/vite-plugin` is build-only and should be in
    80→  `devDependencies`. `@sentry/node` is needed at runtime by backend/sidecar, correct in `dependencies`.
    81→
    82→## Experimental Feature Toggle Pattern (Confirmed)
    83→
    84→- `experimental_*` fields are `boolean | undefined` on `Settings` — `undefined` means ON (backwards-compat)
    85→- `isTabVisible(tab, settings)` is exported from `ContentTabBar.tsx`
    86→- `effectiveContentTab` in `MainContent` = `isTabVisible(raw, settings) ? raw : "changes"` (was "code" before Changes rename)
    87→- BUG (unfixed): `isTabVisible` returns `settings?.[key] === true`, which returns `false` while settings is loading.
    88→  Correct: treat `undefined` as `true` for experimental tabs (`val === undefined || val === true`).
    89→
    90→## workspaceLayoutStore Setter Inconsistency (Confirmed Bug)
    91→
    92→- `setLayout` does a three-way merge: `{ ...defaultLayout, ...existingLayout, ...updates }` — correct.
    93→- All individual setters (`setActiveContentTab`, `setSelectedFilePath`, etc.) do only a two-way merge:
    94→  `{ ...(state.layouts[id] || defaultLayout), field: value }`. When a record exists but lacks a new field
    95→  (e.g. after a version bump), the individual setters will NOT backfill from `defaultLayout`.
    96→  Fix: use the same three-way pattern in all individual setters.
    97→- Store is now at version 10 (bumped during Files-as-top-level-tab refactor). `migrate` wipes all layouts.
    98→
    99→## Simulator / Rust Command Patterns (Confirmed)
   100→
   101→- `sim_has_xcode_project` is a `fn` (sync) Tauri command — safe ONLY because Pass 1 (filesystem
   102→  scan) dominates. Pattern: split fast probe (`has_xcode_project_fast`) from full build.
   103→- macOS-only Tauri commands: gated via `#[cfg(target_os = "macos")]` in both `commands/mod.rs`
   104→  and `main.rs` invoke_handler lists.
   105→- Three-state probe pattern: `null | true | false`. `null` = loading, suppresses button flicker.
   106→
   107→## Simulator State Machine Patterns (Confirmed)
   108→
   109→- `dispatch()` = validated transition; `setSession()` = recovery bypass for external observation.
   110→- `handleStop()` dispatches STOP first (optimistic), then awaits IPC — avoids UI stuck in "active".
   111→- `STREAM_READY` requires `current.udid === event.udid` — prevents start/stop race.
   112→
   113→## Framer Motion Patterns (Confirmed)
   114→
   115→- `LazyMotion` with `domAnimation` wraps the whole app in `ThemeProvider.tsx` — always use `m`
   116→  (compact alias) instead of `motion`. Using `motion` bypasses lazy loading and pulls in the full
   117→  bundle. Files `AgentQuestionOverlay.tsx` and `PlanApprovalOverlay.tsx` currently use `motion`
   118→  (not `m`) — this is a pre-existing violation, not introduced by new code.
   119→- `useReducedMotion()` is the established pattern for honoring prefers-reduced-motion. Used in
   120→  `RepositoryItem`, `ToolUseBlock`, `ThinkingBlock`, `PastedImageCard`, `PastedTextCard`,
   121→  `CloneRepositoryModal`. New animated components should follow the same pattern.
   122→- `AnimatePresence` exit animation bug: if a component conditionally returns `null` before
   123→  `AnimatePresence` renders, the exit animation never fires. Pattern must be:
   124→  `<AnimatePresence>{condition && <m.div exit={...}>...</m.div>}</AnimatePresence>` — NOT
   125→  `{condition ? <AnimatePresence><m.div>...</m.div></AnimatePresence> : null}`.
   126→- `AnimatePresence` inside `.map()` anti-pattern: placing `<AnimatePresence>` as the return value
   127→  of a `.map()` call (one per item) means the entire boundary is recreated when the item's key
   128→  changes. When the condition flips (e.g., `isEditing` changes from item A to item B), the boundary
   129→  for A is torn down in the same commit as its child — `AnimatePresence` never sees the child
   130→  transition and the exit animation does not fire. Fix: single `<AnimatePresence>` outside the
   131→  `.map()`, with stable keys on each animated child element.
   132→
   133→## Global queryClient Defaults (Confirmed)
   134→
   135→- `refetchOnWindowFocus: false` globally in `queryClient.ts` — critical to prevent typing lag.
   136→  `refetchOnWindowFocus: true` only acceptable for git diff queries (intentional).
   137→
   138→## CORS / External Fetch Patterns
   139→
   140→- Statuspage.io APIs include `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *` — works from WKWebView.
   141→  Third-party dependency: if headers are removed, fetches silently fail with CORS error.
   142→
   143→## Design Token Completeness
   144→
   145→- `bg-accent-green`, `bg-accent-gold`, `bg-accent-red` ARE valid — defined in `@theme`.
   146→- `bg-warning`, `bg-success`, `text-warning` ARE valid — `--color-warning`/`--color-success` in `@theme`.
   147→
   148→## Analytics (PostHog) Patterns
   149→
   150→- Full details in `analytics-patterns.md`
   151→- Consent model: OPT-OUT (`analytics_enabled !== false` = default ON). Comment in `settings.ts` fixed.
   152→- `_enabled` in `track.ts` defaults to `false` (consent-first). No pre-consent tracking window.
   153→- `app_launched` guarded by `appLaunchTracked` ref — fires once per app lifecycle.
   154→- `onboarding_started` guarded by `onboardingTrackedRef` — StrictMode-safe.
   155→- Analytics toggle in GeneralSection.tsx awaits `saveSetting` and reverts on failure.
   156→- `VITE_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_KEY` in `.env.example` is safe to commit — PostHog ingestion keys are write-only.
   157→- `posthog-js/react` is a subpath export of `posthog-js` — no separate dep needed.
   158→
   159→## Content Panel Redesign Pattern (Confirmed)
   160→
   161→- "Code" tab renamed to "Changes". "Files" promoted from sub-tab inside Code to a top-level tab.
   162→- `RightSidePanel` monolith replaced by thin `ContentView` router (`app/layouts/ContentView.tsx`).
   163→- `ChangesView` (`features/workspace/ui/ChangesView.tsx`) is self-contained: fetches its own
   164→  `useFileChanges`, `useUncommittedFiles`, `useLastTurnFiles` data — parent only provides `workspace`.
   165→- `AllFilesDiffViewer`: `hideHeader?: boolean` prop (default false) for embedding in ChangesView.
   166→- Changes layout: AllFilesDiffViewer (75%) | ResizableHandle | DiffFilesTree (25%).
   167→- Files layout: FileViewer (75%) | ResizableHandle | FileBrowserPanel (25%).
   168→- `PersistentTab` (`app/layouts/PersistentTab.tsx`): thin wrapper for always-mounted tabs using
   169→  `pointer-events-none invisible absolute` CSS-hide pattern.
   170→
   171→## Circular Self-Import Pattern (Recurring Risk)
   172→
   173→- `ChangesView.tsx` imports `useFileChanges` et al. from `"@/features/workspace"` — the barrel that
   174→  re-exports `ChangesView` itself. Bundler resolves it, but it is fragile for HMR.
   175→  Fix: import hooks directly from their source (`../api/workspace.queries`, `../hooks/useWorkspaceLayout`).
   176→- Watch for this pattern whenever a component in `features/X/ui/` is also exported from `features/X/index.ts`
   177→  and then imports from `"@/features/X"`.
   178→
   179→## ContentView Data Ownership Pattern
   180→
   181→- `ContentView` is a routing shell — data fetching belongs in leaf tab components, not in the router.
   182→- The `useFileChanges` call inside `ContentView` (for the Files tab) is a leak of this pattern.
   183→  TanStack Query deduplicates the network call, so it is not a perf issue — but ownership is wrong.
   184→  Fix: move into the Files tab branch or into `FileBrowserPanel` itself.
   185→
   186→## Always-Mount CSS Hide Pattern (Confirmed)
   187→
   188→- Browser, Terminal, and Simulator panels use `pointer-events-none invisible absolute` (not `hidden`)
   189→  when inactive — keeps DOM alive so PTY/WebSocket sessions survive tab switches.
   190→- Pattern: `<div className={cn("h-full w-full", activeTab !== "terminal" && "pointer-events-none invisible absolute")}>`
   191→- `offsetParent === null` check in ResizeObserver guards against `fitAddon.fit()` being called when
   192→  terminal container is CSS-hidden (`invisible` sets `visibility: hidden`, which makes `offsetParent` null for
   193→  absolutely-positioned elements but NOT for `invisible` alone — `invisible absolute` together does make
   194→  `offsetParent` null in most cases, but it is fragile; the `offsetWidth/offsetHeight === 0` check is more reliable).
   195→- `partialize` in the layout store strips terminal tab state (tabs/activeId/nextNum) — PTY processes
   196→  don't survive app restarts; zombie tab metadata would reference dead processes.
   197→- The `updateTabs` helper in TerminalPanel captures `nextTerminalNum` from the _render_ snapshot —
   198→  stale-closure risk exists in the `pendingTask`/`pendingCommand` useEffect callbacks because
   199→  `tabs` and `nextTerminalNum` are excluded from their deps arrays (suppressed with eslint-disable).
   200→
   201→## Multi-Workspace Terminal Persistence Pattern (TerminalPanel.tsx)
   202→
   203→- `visitedWorkspaces: Map<workspaceId, workspacePath>` in TerminalPanel state keeps one
   204→  `WorkspaceTerminals` subtree mounted per visited workspace — PTYs survive workspace switches.
   205→- Non-current workspace terminals use same `pointer-events-none invisible absolute h-full w-full`
   206→  CSS-hide pattern inside a `relative` container.
   207→- `WorkspaceTerminals` sub-component uses per-workspace Zustand selectors for isolation.
   208→- Known leak: `visitedWorkspaces` has no eviction path when a workspace is deleted. PTYs for
   209→  deleted workspaces stay alive until TerminalPanel unmounts. Needs workspace deletion event hook.
   210→- `pendingTask` useEffect risk: if task queued just before workspace switch, the tab opens in the
   211→  NEW workspace because `workspaceId` in deps reflects the current workspace at effect fire time.
   212→- Terminal.tsx `initialCommand` is in the main effect dep array but only consumed once at mount.
   213→  Safe in practice because `id` is globally unique (`crypto.randomUUID()`), so effect runs once per terminal.
   214→  Cleaner to exclude `initialCommand` from deps and snapshot it inside the effect.
   215→
   216→## AgentHandler Interface Patterns (Confirmed)
   217→
   218→- Renamed methods: `handleQuery→query`, `handleCancel→cancel`, `handleReset→reset` (completed in #179)
   219→- `AgentCapabilities` struct added to `AgentHandler` interface — all 4 booleans required
   220→- Optional Claude-specific methods on `AgentHandler`: `auth?`, `initWorkspace?`, `getContextUsage?`,
   221→  `updatePermissionMode?` — guarded by optional-chaining at dispatch in `index.ts`
   222→- `ContextUsageParams` in `agent-handler.ts` has shape `{id, options: {cwd, claudeSessionId}}`.
   223→  The call site in `index.ts` passes `request` (type `Omit<ContextUsageRequest, "type">`) which has an
   224→  extra `agentType` field — TypeScript accepts this as structural subtyping, but it means
   225→  `ContextUsageParams` is effectively a subset alias of `ContextUsageRequest`.
   226→- `capabilities` field is declared but never read at dispatch sites — all dispatch guards use optional
   227→  chaining on the method itself (`agent?.auth`), not `agent.capabilities.auth`. The `capabilities`
   228→  object currently serves documentation purposes only (no runtime enforcement).
   229→- `updatePermissionMode` dispatch in `index.ts` silently no-ops when method is absent (no rejection),
   230→  while `auth` and `initWorkspace` reject with an error. Intentionally asymmetric: permission mode
   231→  is fire-and-forget; auth/workspaceInit are request-response.
   232→- Old method name stale references remain in: `claude-session.ts` JSDoc comments (lines 17, 49) and
   233→  three `console.log` strings in `claude-handler.ts` (lines 100, 119, 179). Not runtime bugs.
   234→- Test describe blocks in `claude-handler.test.ts` still use old names: `handleClaudeQuery` (line 144),
   235→  `handleClaudeCancel` (line 768), `handleClaudeUpdatePermissionMode` (line 798).
   236→
   237→## HTTP vs WS Query Parity Gap (PR "Create Workspace from PR or Branch")
   238→
   239→- `GET /workspaces/by-repo` HTTP fallback and query-engine `runQuery("workspaces")` both build `RepoGroup[]` independently.
   240→  When adding a field to one, must add to both. Confirmed: `git_origin_url` added to query-engine but NOT to HTTP route group object.
   241→- Full details in `http-vs-ws-parity.md`
   242→
   243→## createWorkspace Command Return Value (Pre-Existing Bug, #confirmed)
   244→
   245→- `WorkspaceService.create()` returns `as unknown as Workspace` — but the WS ack only has `{ accepted, commandId }`.
   246→- `workspace.id` on the result is `undefined`. `selectWorkspace(undefined)` no-ops silently.
   247→- Pre-existing since bad96ad4 (protocol unification). Does not cause visible breakage because WS subscription pushes snapshot.
   248→- Full details in `create-workspace-command-return.md`
   249→
   250→## Connection State Machine (features/connection)
   251→
   252→- Zustand store in `connectionStore.ts`: CONNECTED → GRACE_PERIOD (2s) → RECONNECTING (30s total) → DISCONNECTED
   253→- `onDisconnected` guard checks `current !== "connected"` — must allow re-entry from `disconnected`
   254→  to handle Retry button flow (forceReconnect fires notifyConnectionChange(false) before new socket opens)
   255→- `forceReconnect()` in platform/ws immediately calls `notifyConnectionChange(false)` — if guard blocks,
   256→  the banner freezes at DISCONNECTED with no recovery until socket connects
   257→- `emitSendAttemptFailed` in `connectionEvents.ts` is a module-level Set-based event bus (no React deps)
   258→- WS error messages that trigger `emitSendAttemptFailed`: both `"not connected"` AND `"disconnected"`
   259→  must be matched — `"WebSocket disconnected"` (from onclose pending-command rejection) will be missed
   260→  if only checking `"not connected"`
   261→- `platform/ws → connectionStore` is safe (no circular import). `session.queries → connectionEvents` is safe.
   262→- `ConnectionBanner` animates `height` (violates project guidelines) — causes panel jitter on 32→44 change
   263→- `ConnectionOrb` color crossfade: CSS `transition-colors` on a class swap is better than Framer Motion
   264→  `backgroundColor` interpolation (avoids paint-per-frame)
   265→
   266→## Mobile Web Layout Patterns
   267→
   268→- Full details in `mobile-layout-patterns.md`
   269→- `MobileLayout` has `key={selectedWorkspace.id}` at call site in MainContent.tsx — remounts on workspace switch, resetting `activeTab` state
   270→- `MobileTab` type defined once in MobileTabBar.tsx, imported in MobileLayout.tsx
   271→- `AllFilesDiffViewer` in mobile code view uses `hideHeader` prop to suppress close button
   272→- `MobileTabBar` safe-area-inset: uses `pb-[env(...)]` which compresses content; needs `min-h` adjustment
   273→- `MobileTabBar` has ARIA semantics: `role="tablist"`, `role="tab"`, `aria-selected`, `aria-controls`, `aria-labelledby`
   274→
   275→## parseContent Type Contract (Confirmed)
   276→
   277→- `parseContent` from `useSessionWithMessages` has type `(content: string) => (ContentBlock | string)[] | string`
   278→  — NOT `ContentBlock[]`. Returns `string` for plain-text user messages and envelope-format cancelled messages.
   279→- Any utility that accepts `parseContent` as a parameter MUST declare the full return type and guard
   280→  with `Array.isArray` before iterating. Declaring a narrower type (`ContentBlock[]`) compiles but
   281→  causes silent data loss at runtime (string is iterated character-by-character, all `isToolUseBlock`
   282→  guards return false).
   283→- Standard guard pattern: `const result = parseContent(msg.content); if (!Array.isArray(result)) continue;`
   284→
   285→## Reverse-Traverse + Flip Pattern for Message Extraction
   286→
   287→- Walking messages in reverse (newest→oldest) and collecting chunks, then calling `.reverse()` to
   288→  restore chronological order is correct at the message level.
   289→- When also walking blocks within each message in reverse, intra-message block order is ALSO reversed
   290→  before the final flip — resulting in wrong per-message block order after `.reverse()`.
   291→- Correct pattern: walk messages in reverse, but walk BLOCKS within each message FORWARD. Either
   292→  `unshift` message-level results to the output array, or accumulate per-message and prepend.
   293→
   294→## TOOL_COLORS Map Completeness
   295→
   296→- Every tool renderer registered in `registerTools.ts` should have a corresponding entry in `TOOL_COLORS`
   297→  in `toolColors.ts`. Missing entries don't cause crashes but leave other components that derive color
   298→  by tool name with `undefined` (falls through to no class).
   299→- When adding a new renderer, add its color entry to `TOOL_COLORS` in the same commit.
   300→## Unread Session Indicator Pattern (Confirmed)
   301→
   302→- Store: `useUnreadStore` in `features/session/store/unreadStore.ts` — Zustand + persist, `unreadSessionIds: Record<string, true>`.
   303→- Mark unread: `useGlobalSessionNotifications` fires on `working → idle|error|needs_response|needs_plan_response`
   304→  transition ONLY when `!isUserViewingSession(ws.id, sessionId)`.
   305→- Mark read: two sites — `handleWorkspaceClick` in MainLayout (sidebar click), and `ChatArea` effect on `activeSessionId` change.
   306→- **Design gap**: `sortByStatusPriority`, `getStatusCounts`, `getRepoPriorityStatus`, `groupByStatus` all call
   307→  `getDisplayStatus(workspace)` WITHOUT `hasUnseenActivity`. Sidebar sorting and collapsed-repo badge color
   308→  never reflect the unread store. Only `WorkspaceItem` renders the correct status.
   309→- **Hook-after-conditional-return bug**: `useUnreadStore` is called at line 112 of `WorkspaceItem.tsx`, AFTER
   310→  the `if (isInitializing) return` early exit at line 52. This violates React Rules of Hooks. The comment
   311→  at line 45 ("Hooks must be called unconditionally") refers only to `useWorkingDuration` — the dev who added
   312→  `useUnreadStore` missed the existing early return.
   313→- **isUserViewingSession fallback**: `!layout.activeChatTabSessionId` means the session is assumed visible
   314→  when no tab session is persisted (new workspace, first session). This is intentional and correct.
   315→- **localStorage leak**: persisted unread state is never pruned for deleted/archived workspaces.
   316→  Benign (orphan keys, ~10 bytes each), but accumulates over time.
   317→- **`unreadSessionIds` full-map subscription in ChatArea**: `useUnreadStore((s) => s.unreadSessionIds)`
   318→  subscribes to the entire map. Any workspace marking unread re-renders ChatArea. Should filter by
   319→  `chatSessionIds` using a stable derived selector to avoid cross-workspace re-renders.
   320→
   321→## See Also
   322→
   323→- `patterns-deep.md` — overflow notes: error classification, chat virtualization, PRStatus, border radius, sidecar resume
   324→- `message-envelope-pattern.md` — session message envelope/flat array patterns
   325→

input.old_string:
## Unread Session Indicator Pattern (Confirmed)

- Store: `useUnreadStore` in `features/session/store/unreadStore.ts` — Zustand + persist, `unreadSessionIds: Record<string, true>`.
- Mark unread: `useGlobalSessionNotifications` fires on `working → idle|error|needs_response|needs_plan_response`
  transition ONLY when `!isUserViewingSession(ws.id, sessionId)`.
- Mark read: two sites — `handleWorkspaceClick` in MainLayout (sidebar click), and `ChatArea` effect on `activeSessionId` change.
- **Design gap**: `sortByStatusPriority`, `getStatusCounts`, `getRepoPriorityStatus`, `groupByStatus` all call
  `getDisplayStatus(workspace)` WITHOUT `hasUnseenActivity`. Sidebar sorting and collapsed-repo badge color
  never reflect the unread store. Only `WorkspaceItem` renders the correct status.
- **Hook-after-conditional-return bug**: `useUnreadStore` is called at line 112 of `WorkspaceItem.tsx`, AFTER
  the `if (isInitializing) return` early exit at line 52. This violates React Rules of Hooks. The comment
  at line 45 ("Hooks must be called unconditionally") refers only to `useWorkingDuration` — the dev who added
  `useUnreadStore` missed the existing early return.
- **isUserViewingSession fallback**: `!layout.activeChatTabSessionId` means the session is assumed visible
  when no tab session is persisted (new workspace, first session). This is intentional and correct.
- **localStorage leak**: persisted unread state is never pruned for deleted/archived workspaces.
  Benign (orphan keys, ~10 bytes each), but accumulates over time.
- **`unreadSessionIds` full-map subscription in ChatArea**: `useUnreadStore((s) => s.unreadSessionIds)`
  subscribes to the entire map. Any workspace marking unread re-renders ChatArea. Should filter by
  `chatSessionIds` using a stable derived selector to avoid cross-workspace re-renders.
input.new_string:
## Unread Session Indicator Pattern (Confirmed)

- Store: `useUnreadStore` in `features/session/store/unreadStore.ts` — Zustand + persist, `unreadSessionIds: Record<string, true>`.
- Mark unread: `useGlobalSessionNotifications` fires on `working → idle|error|needs_response|needs_plan_response`
  transition ONLY when `!isUserViewingSession(ws.id, sessionId)`.
- Mark read: two sites — `handleWorkspaceClick` in MainLayout (sidebar click), and `handleTabChangeWithRead`
  in ChatArea (tab switch).
- **String-key serialization hack in RepositoryItem**: builds a comma-joined string of unread session IDs
  from the store, then splits it back to a Set in useMemo. This is an anti-pattern. Fix: `useShallow` selector
  that filters in the selector itself and returns a `Set<string>`. Saves ~18 lines.
- **ChatArea full-map subscription**: `useUnreadStore((s) => s.unreadSessionIds)` subscribes to the entire
  map — any workspace marking unread re-renders ChatArea. Fix: `useShallow` filtered to `chatSessionIds`.
- **`Record<string, true>` vs `Set`**: Record is used because JSON doesn't natively serialize Set. The
  tradeoff is real, but should be documented. Extract `isSessionUnread(s, id) => !!s.unreadSessionIds[id]`
  helper so callers don't all inline `!!s.unreadSessionIds[id]`.
- **`unreadActions` is redundant indirection**: every method delegates to `useUnreadStore.getState()`.
  `useUnreadStore.getState().markRead(id)` is already stable; the wrapper adds no value. `isUnread` is
  never called from `unreadActions`. Consider deleting.
- **Dead functions in status.ts**: `getUnreadCount`, `groupByStatus`, `getStatusCounts`, `getRepoUnreadCount`,
  `getRepoPriorityStatus` are exported but have zero import sites. Safe to delete.
- **isUserViewingSession naming**: reads from Zustand/layout snapshots (non-reactive), not a hook.
  Rename to `checkIsViewingSession` to avoid hook-name confusion.
- **Hook-after-conditional-return bug (pre-existing, UNFIXED)**: `useUnreadStore` at WorkspaceItem.tsx line 52
  is AFTER the `if (isInitializing) return` at the same line. Violates Rules of Hooks.
- **isUserViewingSession fallback**: `!layout.activeChatTabSessionId` means the session is assumed visible
  when no tab session is persisted (new workspace, first session). Intentional and correct.
- **localStorage leak**: persisted unread state is never pruned for deleted/archived workspaces.
  Benign (orphan keys, ~10 bytes each), but accumulates over time.

input.content:
---
name: Over-engineering anti-patterns to flag
description: User explicitly wants reviews to catch unnecessary complexity, indirection, and clever-but-harder-to-read patterns
type: feedback
---

Flag anything where a simpler approach achieves the same result. The user has already caught:
1. String-key serialization hack in a Zustand selector (build comma string → split back to Set) instead of `useShallow`
2. A `useEffect` syncing a value into a ref when a callback wrapper was cleaner
3. Pass-through `unreadActions` wrapper that just delegates to `useUnreadStore.getState()` with no added value

**Why:** The user wants to be shown simpler alternatives every time, not just when the code is "wrong". "Could this be 3 lines instead of 10?" is the bar.

**How to apply:**
- For every Zustand selector that returns a derived object or Set, ask: could `useShallow` replace a string-key or useMemo workaround?
- For every `useEffect` that only syncs a value (no async, no side-effect beyond ref update), ask: is this just a render-body assignment?
- For every re-export wrapper file or `Actions` object, ask: does it add anything over direct `getState()` calls?
- For every exported function in a utility file, check it has at least one import site. Dead exports in central files mislead readers.
- Redundant indirection (A calls B which calls C where B adds nothing) should always be flagged.

message.content: Okay, so I tested it and I'm not sure. So imagine I open a workspace and I have multiple sessions. I send a message on the multiple sessions and go away. If I go back to the workspace and this one session is opened, I haven't opened the rest. So the unseen activity dot in a workspace sidebar and also in the sessions should still be there, you know, maybe explore what I'm saying, what is the problem because I haven't described it properly, but like, I think you know what I'm saying, explore.

content.thinking:
The user is saying that when they have a workspace with multiple sessions (tabs), send messages to multiple sessions, then navigate away — when they come back to the workspace, only the active tab's session gets marked as read. But the sidebar workspace dot should still show as unread because there are other sessions in that workspace that haven't been read yet.

Let me think about the current flow:

1. User has workspace W with sessions S1 (active tab), S2, S3
2. User sends messages to S1, S2, S3 (all go to "working")
3. User navigates away to another workspace


4. All three sessions finish and get marked as unread in the store
5. User clicks workspace W in the sidebar
6. `handleWorkspaceClick` marks only the active session (S1) as read
7. ChatArea mounts but `handleTabChangeWithRead` doesn't fire since there's no tab change
8. The sidebar shows workspace W, but the status is wrong

The issue is that the WorkspaceItem in the sidebar only checks if the current session has unread activity, not whether any of the other sessions in that workspace have unread messages.

Checking unread status...

The sidebar needs to know about all sessions in the workspace's tabs, not just the current one. I could either pass all session IDs to WorkspaceItem or have it read directly from the layout store to get the full list of sessions. I need to look at the actual code to verify how the workspace and session data flows through the components, particularly checking what information is available in the layout store and how WorkspaceItem can access the unread state for all sessions in a workspace.

content.text:
I think the issue is: the sidebar workspace dot only checks `current_session_id` against the unread store, but a workspace can have multiple sessions open in tabs. The user is saying that when they have multiple session tabs in a workspace and one session finishes (goes from working to idle), the tab doesn't show the unread dot. They're looking at the workspace while the sessions are running, so the issue is about the tab-level unread indicator not appearing.

Let me think about the flow:

1. User has workspace W open with sessions S1 (active tab), S2, S3
2. User sends messages to all three — all go to "working" state
3. S2 finishes (working → idle)
4. The user is currently viewing this workspace, with S1 as the active tab


5. The question is whether S2 gets marked as unread

I'm looking at the notification logic to see how it determines whether to mark a session as unread when it transitions from working to idle. The code checks if the user is currently viewing that specific session, and only marks it unread if they're not viewing it.

Tracing through the scenario: the user is in workspace W viewing S1, and when S2 finishes, the check compares the active tab (S1) against S2 and finds they don't match, so S2 should get marked as unread.

But I'm noticing something off in the notification hook itself. Let me look at how it's subscribing to query cache updates and processing workspace data...

Examining notification logic...

The core issue is that the hook only tracks the workspace's `current_session_id` and its corresponding `session_status`. When a workspace has multiple sessions across tabs, only the primary session's status changes trigger notifications—the other sessions' status updates are completely invisible because the workspace query doesn't include their individual statuses. So S2's completion goes undetected by this hook.

The solution is to also monitor the individual session detail queries that `useWorkingSessionIds` subscribes to, since those contain the actual status information for all sessions in the workspace, not just the canonical one.

Non-primary sessions (those open in other tabs but not the workspace's canonical session) have their statuses tracked via individual `["sessions", "detail", id]` queries.

So the fix is: also watch the session detail queries in the cache subscription. When any session detail query updates with a status change from "working" to a terminal state, mark it as unread.

content.text:
Clean. Now the flow works for multi-session tabs:

1. User has workspace W with tabs S1 (active), S2, S3 — all working
2. S2 finishes: `["sessions", "detail", S2]` cache updates → new branch detects `working → idle` → `checkIsViewingSession(W, S2)` → active tab is S1, not S2 → marks S2 as unread
3. Tab bar shows unread dot on S2 tab
4. Sidebar shows gold dot (because S2 is in the workspace's `chatTabSessionIds` and is unread)
5. S3 finishes similarly → S3 also marked unread, dot on S3 tab
6. User clicks S2 tab → `handleTabChangeWithRead` marks S2 as read → dot disappears from S2
7. S3 still unread → sidebar dot stays until user clicks S3 tab

The workspace-level watcher still handles the `current_session_id` transitions (for OS notifications, analytics, etc.), while the new session-detail watcher catches all individual sessions for unread marking.

Or I could track which sessions were actually working when the user last left this workspace and persist that in the layout store. But that's getting complicated. Let me just go with tracking the previous working set and marking sessions as unread when they stop being worked on.

Still writing the effect logic... I see the issue—when switching workspaces, the ref doesn't reset since ChatArea stays mounted, so I'd be comparing the old workspace's sessions against the new one's. I need to track the workspace ID and reset both refs when it changes, then iterate through the previous sessions to detect which ones finished.

Still writing the comparison logic...

The tradeoff here is that unread markers won't appear for inactive sessions in unselected workspaces, but since the current session always gets caught by the notification hook, this only affects extra tabs—which feels like the right balance. I'll go with this approach.

content.text:
Actually wait — I realize the mount-seed approach has a worse bug. If the user reads S2, switches away, and comes back — it re-marks S2 as unread because it's "started but not working." We can't distinguish "finished while away" from "user already read this."

Let me revert to the simple approach and just handle workspace switches properly instead:

lines: -  // Mark sessions as unread when they stop working and aren't the active tab.
-  // On first mount, seed with all started sessions so we catch sessions
-  // that finished while this workspace wasn't selected.
+  // Mark non-active tab sessions as unread when they leave the working set.
-      // First run: include all started sessions to catch offscreen finishes
+    // Reset on workspace switch — don't compare across workspaces
// Unread tracking: read the full map and filter to this workspace's sessions.

content.text:
There is one remaining gap: if the user is on workspace A and workspace B has non-current sessions (extra tabs beyond the primary) that finish — those won't get marked unread because `ChatArea` for B isn't mounted.

The `current_session_id` is always covered by the notification hook. It's only the **extra** tab sessions in a **non-selected** workspace that have this gap. In practice this is rare — the user would need to: open multiple tabs, start agents on all of them, switch to a different workspace, then come back.

If you want to cover that too, the cleanest way would be to persist the working session IDs per workspace in the layout store when the user navigates away, so when they come back we can detect what changed. But that adds more code. Want me to handle that, or is the current coverage good enough?

content.thinking:
The user is describing a specific scenario:

1. They have a workspace open with session S1 (active tab)
2. They send a message to S1 (it starts working)
3. They open/switch to session S2 tab and work with it
4. S1 finishes in the background (while they're on S2 tab)
5. When they click back to S1, they see it's done — but there was never an unread dot on S1's tab

The issue: S1 finishes while the user is on S2, but S1's tab never shows the unread indicator.


Let me trace through what should happen: when S1 finishes and transitions from working to idle, the effect should detect that S1 is no longer in the working set while S2 is active, then call `markUnread(S1)`. The logic appears sound, but the user reports it's not working, so I need to examine the actual implementation more carefully.

I'm wondering if `useWorkingSessionIds` is properly tracking S1's status even when the user has switched to S2's tab — that could be where the breakdown is happening.

content.text: Let me trace through exactly what happens in that scenario. The logic should catch it, so something else is wrong.

input.prompt:
I need to trace the full unread indicator flow for this scenario:
1. User has workspace open, session S1 active tab, sends message → S1 goes to "working"
2. User opens/switches to session S2 tab
3. S1 finishes (working → idle) while user is on S2
4. S1's tab should show unread dot — but it doesn't

Read these files and trace the data flow:

1. `apps/web/src/features/session/hooks/useWorkingSessionIds.ts` — how does it track which sessions are working?
2. `apps/web/src/features/session/hooks/useGlobalSessionNotifications.ts` — the notification hook
3. `apps/web/src/app/layouts/ChatArea.tsx` — the unread marking logic (around line 65-90)
4. `apps/web/src/features/session/store/` — the unread store
5. `apps/web/src/features/workspace/ui/MainContentTabs.tsx` — how tabs render unread dots
6. `apps/web/src/features/sidebar/ui/WorkspaceItem.tsx` — how sidebar renders unread dots
7. `apps/web/src/features/sidebar/lib/status.ts` — status/indicator logic

For each file, read the full contents. Look for:

- When the active tab changes, does the query for the previous session get unsubscribed?
- Is the unread store's `markUnread` actually being called?
- How does the tab render the unread dot — does it read from the unread store correctly?
- Any race conditions or missing dependencies in the useEffect?

Report back the exact data flow and where it breaks.

content.text:
Excellent. Now I have all the pieces. Let me trace through the complete flow and identify the break point. Let me create a summary document of the findings:

## FULL UNREAD INDICATOR FLOW ANALYSIS

### THE SCENARIO
1. User has workspace open, session S1 active tab, sends message → S1 goes to "working"
2. User opens/switches to session S2 tab
3. S1 finishes (working → idle) while user is on S2
4. Expected: S1's tab should show unread dot
5. Actual: S1's tab does NOT show unread dot

---

### DATA FLOW TRACE

#### 1. **How Working Status is Tracked** (`useWorkingSessionIds.ts:82-102`)
- `useWorkingSessionIds(sessionIds: string[])` creates **per-tab queries** for each session in `sessionIds`
- Each query subscribes to `queryKeys.sessions.detail(id)` and selects only the status
- Returns a Set of session IDs where `status === "working"`

**Key point:** This hook is called in `ChatArea.tsx:63` with `chatSessionIds` — the session IDs from all open tabs.

#### 2. **When Session Finishes** (`useGlobalSessionNotifications.ts:113-125`)
The notification hook watches the `workspaces` by-repo cache and when it detects `prevStatus === "working"` and the new status is `"idle"` (or error/needs_response):

```typescript
if (prevStatus === "working" &&
    (status === "idle" || status === "error" || status === "needs_response" || status === "needs_plan_response")
) {
  const isViewingSession = checkIsViewingSession(ws.id, sessionId);
  if (!isViewingSession) {
    unreadActions.markUnread(sessionId);  // ← Mark unread in store
  }
}
```

This calls `unreadActions.markUnread(sessionId)` **only if the user is NOT currently viewing that session**.

#### 3. **How Unread Detection Works** (`ChatArea.tsx:65-83`)
The `ChatArea` component also has fallback logic to mark sessions unread when they leave the working set:

```typescript
const workingSessionIds = useWorkingSessionIds(chatSessionIds);
const prevWorkingRef = useRef(workingSessionIds);

useEffect(() => {
  const prev = prevWorkingRef.current;
  prevWorkingRef.current = workingSessionIds;
  for (const sid of prev) {
    if (!workingSessionIds.has(sid) && sid !== activeSessionId) {
      unreadActions.markUnread(sid);
    }
  }
}, [workingSessionIds, activeSessionId, workspace.id]);
```

This marks a session unread when it **leaves the working set** AND it's **not the active session**.

#### 4. **How Unread is Displayed** (`MainContentTabBar.tsx:134-139`)
```typescript
const isUnread =
  !isActive &&
  !isWorking &&
  !!tab.data?.sessionId &&
  unreadSessionIds.has(tab.data.sessionId);
```

A tab shows the unread dot when:
- Tab is NOT active
- Tab is NOT working
- Session ID exists
- Session ID is in the unread store

---

### THE BREAK POINT: RACE CONDITION

Here's where it breaks:

**Scenario:**
1. User is on S2 (active)
2. S1 finishes working (idle) while user is on S2
3. The workspace cache gets updated via WS

**What happens:**

In `useGlobalSessionNotifications.ts:93-94`:
```typescript
// Detect session + setup transitions from the workspace query cache.
// Only watches current_session_id per workspace. Non-current sessions
// (extra tabs) are handled by ChatArea which tracks workingSessionIds.
```

The notification hook **only watches `current_session_id`**, which is the workspace's **single active session** (NOT the tab-specific session). The comment says "extra tabs are handled by ChatArea" — but this is where the problem is.

### THE ROOT CAUSE: `current_session_id` Conflict with Multiple Tabs

The workspace has a `current_session_id` field, which is typically set to the **last active session** or the **workspace's primary session**. When:

1. User opens S2 (switches tab)
2. S1 finishes while user is on S2
3. The `current_session_id` might still be pointing to S1 (not S2)

The notification hook detects S1 finishing BUT:
- It checks `checkIsViewingSession(ws.id, sessionId)` using `activeChatTabSessionId` from layout
- If the layout's `activeChatTabSessionId` is correctly set to S2's ID, then `isViewingSession` returns FALSE
- So it **should** call `unreadActions.markUnread(S1)`

**BUT** — the issue is in `ChatArea.tsx:59-63`:

```typescript
const chatSessionIds = useMemo(
  () => tabs.filter((t) => t.data?.sessionId).map((t) => t.data!.sessionId!),
  [tabs]
);
const workingSessionIds = useWorkingSessionIds(chatSessionIds);
```

This queries **all tabs' sessions**. But if the `workingSessionIds` subscription is **not keeping the S1 session's detail cache fresh**, then when S1 finishes, the `useQueries` in `useWorkingSessionIds` might **not receive the status update in time**.

### THE ACTUAL ISSUE: WS Subscription Timing

Looking at `useWorkingSessionIds` (session.queries.ts:82-102):

```typescript
export function useWorkingSessionIds(sessionIds: string[]): Set<string> {
  const queries = useQueries({
    queries: sessionIds.map((id) => ({
      queryKey: queryKeys.sessions.detail(id),
      queryFn: () => SessionService.fetchById(id),
      staleTime: 30_000,
      select: (data: Session): SessionStatus => data.status,
    })),
  });
```

There's **NO `useQuerySubscription` call** in this hook. It only creates raw `useQueries` with HTTP fallback.

Compare to `useSession` (session.queries.ts:54-68):

```typescript
export function useSession(sessionId: string | null) {
  useQuerySubscription("session", {  // ← WS subscription!
    queryKey: queryKeys.sessions.detail(sessionId || ""),
    params: { sessionId: sessionId || "" },
    enabled: !!sessionId,
  });
  // Then useQuery...
}
```

**`useSession` has the WS subscription. `useWorkingSessionIds` does NOT.**

---

### THE SMOKING GUN

In `ChatArea.tsx`, the non-active tabs' sessions are **not subscribed** to the WS `"session"` resource. They only have queries created by `useQueries` in `useWorkingSessionIds`, which rely on HTTP fallback with `staleTime: 30_000`.

**Timeline:**
1. S2 becomes active → `useSession(S2Id)` is called with WS subscription ✅
2. S2's detail cache gets WS pushes ✅
3. S1 is in a tab but is NOT active → `useWorkingSessionIds` creates a raw query for S1 ❌
4. S1 finishes (backend updates session_status) ❌
5. WS pushes `q:snapshot` for session `S1` to subscribers of the `"session"` resource ❌
6. But `useWorkingSessionIds` never subscribed → doesn't receive the push ❌
7. `useWorkingSessionIds` relies on HTTP, which happens on a 30s stale timer or on demand ❌
8. The unread dot is never marked because `workingSessionIds` still contains S1 from the previous render ❌

---

### WHERE IT BREAKS: File Locations

1. **`apps/web/src/features/session/api/session.queries.ts:82-102`** (`useWorkingSessionIds`)
   - Missing WS subscription for non-active tab sessions
   - Only creates raw `useQueries` without subscription setup

2. **`apps/web/src/app/layouts/ChatArea.tsx:59-83`**
   - Calls `useWorkingSessionIds(chatSessionIds)` which doesn't keep non-active sessions fresh
   - The effect at line 69-83 tries to catch sessions leaving the working set, but if the cache never gets updated, it never fires

3. **`apps/web/src/features/session/hooks/useGlobalSessionNotifications.ts:93-94`**
   - Comment claims "extra tabs are handled by ChatArea" but that logic is incomplete
   - Only watches `current_session_id`, not all tab sessions

---

### THE FIX NEEDED

**Option 1 (Correct approach):**
Add WS subscription to `useWorkingSessionIds` so all tab sessions stay fresh:

```typescript
export function useWorkingSessionIds(sessionIds: string[]): Set<string> {
  // Subscribe each session to WS
  sessionIds.forEach((id) => {
    useQuerySubscription("session", {
      queryKey: queryKeys.sessions.detail(id),
      params: { sessionId: id },
      enabled: !!id,
    });
  });
  
  // Then create queries
  const queries = useQueries({...});
  ...
}
```

**Option 2 (Workaround):**
Reduce `staleTime` on the `useQueries` to force HTTP refetches more frequently, or add explicit polling for non-active sessions.

---

### SUMMARY

The unread indicator fails because:

1. **Non-active tab sessions are not WS-subscribed** — they rely on stale HTTP queries
2. When S1 finishes while user is on S2, the WS sends `q:snapshot` for S1 to subscribers of `"session"` resource
3. But `useWorkingSessionIds` never subscribed to S1's status changes
4. The S1 cache stays stale, `workingSessionIds` never changes, the unread effect never fires
5. The tab never shows the unread dot

The data flow breaks at **`useWorkingSessionIds` missing WS subscription setup**.


The notification hook **only watches `current_session_id`**, which is the workspace's **single active session** (NOT the tab-specific session). The comment says "extra tabs are handled by ChatArea" — but this is where the problem is.

### THE ROOT CAUSE: `current_session_id` Conflict with Multiple Tabs

The workspace has a `current_session_id` field, which is typically set to the **last active session** or the **workspace's primary session**. When:

1. User opens S2 (switches tab)
2. S1 finishes while user is on S2
3. The `current_session_id` might still be pointing to S1 (not S2)

The notification hook detects S1 finishing BUT:
- It checks `checkIsViewingSession(ws.id, sessionId)` using `activeChatTabSessionId` from layout
- If the layout's `activeChatTabSessionId` is correctly set to S2's ID, then `isViewingSession` returns FALSE
- So it **should** call `unreadActions.markUnread(S1)`

**BUT** — the issue is in `ChatArea.tsx:59-63`:

```typescript
const chatSessionIds = useMemo(
  () => tabs.filter((t) => t.data?.sessionId).map((t) => t.data!.sessionId!),
  [tabs]
);
const workingSessionIds = useWorkingSessionIds(chatSessionIds);
```

This queries **all tabs' sessions**. But if the `workingSessionIds` subscription is **not keeping the S1 session's detail cache fresh**, then when S1 finishes, the `useQueries` in `useWorkingSessionIds` might **not receive the status update in time**.

### THE ACTUAL ISSUE: WS Subscription Timing

Looking at `useWorkingSessionIds` (session.queries.ts:82-102):

```typescript
export function useWorkingSessionIds(sessionIds: string[]): Set<string> {
  const queries = useQueries({
    queries: sessionIds.map((id) => ({
      queryKey: queryKeys.sessions.detail(id),
      queryFn: () => SessionService.fetchById(id),
      staleTime: 30_000,
      select: (data: Session): SessionStatus => data.status,
    })),
  });
```

There's **NO `useQuerySubscription` call** in this hook. It only creates raw `useQueries` with HTTP fallback.

Compare to `useSession` (session.queries.ts:54-68):

```typescript
export function useSession(sessionId: string | null) {
  useQuerySubscription("session", {  // ← WS subscription!
    queryKey: queryKeys.sessions.detail(sessionId || ""),
    params: { sessionId: sessionId || "" },
    enabled: !!sessionId,
  });
  // Then useQuery...
}
```

**`useSession` has the WS subscription. `useWorkingSessionIds` does NOT.**

---

### THE SMOKING GUN

In `ChatArea.tsx`, the non-active tabs' sessions are **not subscribed** to the WS `"session"` resource. They only have queries created by `useQueries` in `useWorkingSessionIds`, which rely on HTTP fallback with `staleTime: 30_000`.

**Timeline:**
1. S2 becomes active → `useSession(S2Id)` is called with WS subscription ✅
2. S2's detail cache gets WS pushes ✅
3. S1 is in a tab but is NOT active → `useWorkingSessionIds` creates a raw query for S1 ❌
4. S1 finishes (backend updates session_status) ❌
5. WS pushes `q:snapshot` for session `S1` to subscribers of the `"session"` resource ❌
6. But `useWorkingSessionIds` never subscribed → doesn't receive the push ❌
7. `useWorkingSessionIds` relies on HTTP, which happens on a 30s stale timer or on demand ❌
8. The unread dot is never marked because `workingSessionIds` still contains S1 from the previous render ❌

---

### WHERE IT BREAKS: File Locations

1. **`apps/web/src/features/session/api/session.queries.ts:82-102`** (`useWorkingSessionIds`)
   - Missing WS subscription for non-active tab sessions
   - Only creates raw `useQueries` without subscription setup

2. **`apps/web/src/app/layouts/ChatArea.tsx:59-83`**
   - Calls `useWorkingSessionIds(chatSessionIds)` which doesn't keep non-active sessions fresh
   - The effect at line 69-83 tries to catch sessions leaving the working set, but if the cache never gets updated, it never fires

3. **`apps/web/src/features/session/hooks/useGlobalSessionNotifications.ts:93-94`**
   - Comment claims "extra tabs are handled by ChatArea" but that logic is incomplete
   - Only watches `current_session_id`, not all tab sessions

---

### THE FIX NEEDED

**Option 1 (Correct approach):**
Add WS subscription to `useWorkingSessionIds` so all tab sessions stay fresh:

```typescript
export function useWorkingSessionIds(sessionIds: string[]): Set<string> {
  // Subscribe each session to WS
  sessionIds.forEach((id) => {
    useQuerySubscription("session", {
      queryKey: queryKeys.sessions.detail(id),
      params: { sessionId: id },
      enabled: !!id,
    });
  });
  
  // Then create queries
  const queries = useQueries({...});
  ...
}
```

**Option 2 (Workaround):**
Reduce `staleTime` on the `useQueries` to force HTTP refetches more frequently, or add explicit polling for non-active sessions.

---

### SUMMARY

The unread indicator fails because:

1. **Non-active tab sessions are not WS-subscribed** — they rely on stale HTTP queries
2. When S1 finishes while user is on S2, the WS sends `q:snapshot` for S1 to subscribers of `"session"` resource
3. But `useWorkingSessionIds` never subscribed to S1's status changes
4. The S1 cache stays stale, `workingSessionIds` never changes, the unread effect never fires
5. The tab never shows the unread dot

The data flow breaks at **`useWorkingSessionIds` missing WS subscription setup**.

1→/**
     2→ * Sidebar Status Configuration System
     3→ *
     4→ * Centralized configuration for workspace/session statuses, priorities, and visual styling.
     5→ * Design philosophy: Make it easy to iterate on colors and priorities without touching component code.
     6→ *
     7→ * @see src/features/session/types.ts for SessionStatus type definition

    10→import type { Workspace } from "@/features/workspace/types";
    11→import type { WorkspaceStatus } from "@shared/enums";
    12→
    13→/**
    14→ * Display status extends SessionStatus with derived states.
    15→ * 'unread' is derived from needs_response / needs_plan_response session status.
    16→ */
    17→export type DisplayStatus = "idle" | "working" | "error" | "unread";
    18→
    19→/**
    20→ * Priority levels for sorting (higher = more urgent)
    21→ */
    22→export enum StatusPriority {
    23→  ERROR = 4, // Critical - something broke
    24→  UNREAD = 3, // Important - needs review / response
    25→  WORKING = 2, // Active - in progress
    26→  IDLE = 0, // Dormant - no activity
    27→}
    28→
    29→/**
    30→ * Visual configuration for each status
    31→ *
    32→ * Design System:
    33→ * - badge: Color for notification badges (collapsed sidebar)
    34→ * - border: Border/glow color for active states
    35→ * - text: Text color for status labels
    36→ * - bg: Background color for sections/cards
    37→ *
    38→ * Color Semantics:
    39→ * 🔴 Red (error) = Critical, blocking, needs immediate action
    40→ * 🟡 Amber (unread) = Important, needs attention when ready
    41→ * 🟢 Green (working) = Active, positive, making progress
    42→ * 🟣 Purple (compacting) = Transient, maintenance, ignore
    43→ * ⚪ Gray (idle) = Dormant, background, low priority
    44→ */
    45→export interface StatusConfig {
    46→  priority: StatusPriority;
    47→  label: string;
    48→  labelActive: string; // Present continuous form (e.g., "Working...")
    49→  badge: string; // Tailwind classes for badge
    50→  border: string; // Tailwind classes for border/glow
    51→  text: string; // Tailwind classes for text

    55→
    56→/**
    57→ * Status configuration map
    58→ *
    59→ * IMPORTANT: To change colors, edit this object (no component changes needed)
    60→ */
    61→export const STATUS_CONFIG: Record<DisplayStatus, StatusConfig> = {
    62→  error: {
    63→    priority: StatusPriority.ERROR,
    64→    label: "Error",
    65→    labelActive: "Error",
    66→    badge: "bg-destructive text-destructive-foreground",
    67→    border: "border-destructive/60",

    69→    bg: "bg-destructive/10",

    71→  },

    73→    priority: StatusPriority.UNREAD,
    74→    label: "Needs Attention",
    75→    labelActive: "Awaiting Input",
    76→    badge: "bg-status-unread text-status-unread-fg",
    77→    border: "border-status-unread/60",
