    79→    bg: "bg-status-unread/10",
    80→    pulse: false,
    81→  },
    82→  working: {
    83→    priority: StatusPriority.WORKING,
    84→    label: "Working",
    85→    labelActive: "Working...",
    86→    badge: "bg-primary text-primary-foreground",
    87→    border: "border-primary/60",
    88→    text: "text-primary",
    89→    bg: "bg-primary/10",
    90→    pulse: true,
    91→  },
    92→  idle: {
    93→    priority: StatusPriority.IDLE,
    94→    label: "Idle",
    95→    labelActive: "Idle",
    96→    badge: "bg-muted text-muted-foreground",
    97→    border: "border-muted",
    98→    text: "text-muted-foreground",
    99→    bg: "bg-muted/30",
   100→    pulse: false,
   101→  },
   102→};
   103→
   104→// ── Workflow Status (Linear-style) ────────────────────────────────────────
   105→// Separate from DisplayStatus which shows real-time agent activity.
   106→// Rendered as a small icon to the left of the workspace title.
   107→
   108→export interface WorkflowStatusConfig {
   109→  label: string;
   110→  color: string;
   111→}
   112→
   113→export const WORKFLOW_STATUS_CONFIG: Record<WorkspaceStatus, WorkflowStatusConfig> = {
   114→  backlog: { label: "Backlog", color: "text-muted-foreground" },
   115→  "in-progress": { label: "In Progress", color: "text-status-in-progress" },
   116→  "in-review": { label: "In Review", color: "text-status-in-review" },
   117→  done: { label: "Done", color: "text-status-done" },
   118→  canceled: { label: "Canceled", color: "text-muted-foreground" },
   119→};
   120→
   121→/**
   122→ * Derive display status from workspace data
   123→ *
   124→ * Priority logic:
   125→ * 1. Error — session in error state
   126→ * 2. Unread — agent needs user response OR has unseen activity
   127→ * 3. Working — agent actively processing
   128→ * 4. Idle — dormant
   129→ *
   130→ * @param workspace Workspace data with session info
   131→ * @param hasUnseenActivity Whether the session has activity the user hasn't viewed yet
   132→ * @returns Derived display status for UI rendering
   133→ */
   134→export function getDisplayStatus(workspace: Workspace, hasUnseenActivity = false): DisplayStatus {
   135→  const status = workspace.session_status;
   136→
   137→  if (status === "error") return "error";
   138→  if (status === "needs_response" || status === "needs_plan_response") return "unread";
   139→  if (status === "working") return "working";
   140→  if (hasUnseenActivity) return "unread";
   141→  return "idle";
   142→}
   143→
   144→/**
   145→ * Sort workspaces by status priority
   146→ * Higher priority statuses appear first
   147→ *
   148→ * @param workspaces Array of workspaces to sort
   149→ * @param unreadSessionIds Optional set of session IDs with unseen activity
   150→ * @returns Sorted array (original array not mutated)
   151→ */
   152→export function sortByStatusPriority<T extends Workspace>(
   153→  workspaces: T[],
   154→  unreadSessionIds?: Set<string>
   155→): T[] {
   156→  return [...workspaces].sort((a, b) => {
   157→    const hasUnreadA = !!(a.current_session_id && unreadSessionIds?.has(a.current_session_id));
   158→    const hasUnreadB = !!(b.current_session_id && unreadSessionIds?.has(b.current_session_id));
   159→    const statusA = getDisplayStatus(a, hasUnreadA);
   160→    const statusB = getDisplayStatus(b, hasUnreadB);
   161→    const priorityA = STATUS_CONFIG[statusA].priority;
   162→    const priorityB = STATUS_CONFIG[statusB].priority;
   163→
   164→    // Higher priority first
   165→    if (priorityA !== priorityB) {
   166→      return priorityB - priorityA;
   167→    }
   168→
   169→    // Same priority: sort by latest activity (most recent first)
   170→    const timeA = new Date(a.updated_at).getTime();
   171→    const timeB = new Date(b.updated_at).getTime();
   172→    return timeB - timeA;
   173→  });
   174→}

content.text:
Clean. Here's the summary of what was broken and what I fixed:

**Root cause:** `useWorkingSessionIds` had no WebSocket subscription — it relied on HTTP queries with 30s stale time. So when a non-active tab session finished (working → idle), the cache never got the status update, the transition was never detected, and the unread dot never appeared.

**Fix in `session.queries.ts`:** Added WS subscriptions for ALL tab session IDs directly in `useWorkingSessionIds`. Now every tab session gets real-time status pushes via WebSocket, not just the active one. Also changed `staleTime` to `Infinity` since WS handles freshness.

**Fix in `ChatArea.tsx`:** Added workspace-switch detection (`prevWorkspaceRef`) so the working-set comparison resets when the user switches workspaces, avoiding false transitions across different workspaces.

**Sidebar (`WorkspaceItem.tsx`):** Already checks all tab session IDs (via `chatTabSessionIds` from the layout store), not just `current_session_id`. This was already correct.

**MainLayout `handleWorkspaceClick`:** Already correctly marks only the active tab's session as read. Other tabs keep their unread dots.

The remaining edge case (non-current sessions in non-selected workspaces finishing) still exists but is narrow — the notification hook handles `current_session_id`, and now `useWorkingSessionIds` handles all tab sessions when the workspace IS selected.

I need to trace the full unread indicator flow for this scenario:
1. User has workspace open, session S1 active tab, sends message → S1 goes to "working"
2. User opens/switches to session S2 tab
3. S1 finishes (working → idle) while user is on S2
4. S1's tab should show unread dot — but it doesn't

Read these files and trace the data flow:

1. `apps/web/src/features/session/hooks/useWorkingSessionIds.ts` — how does it track which sessions are working?
2. `apps/web/src/features/session/hooks/useGlobalSessionNotifications.ts` — the notification hook
3. `apps/web/src/app/layouts/ChatArea.tsx` — the unread marking logic (around line 65-90)
4. `apps/web/src/features/session/store/` — the unread store
5. `apps/web/src/features/workspace/ui/MainContentTabs.tsx` — how tabs render unread dots
6. `apps/web/src/features/sidebar/ui/WorkspaceItem.tsx` — how sidebar renders unread dots
7. `apps/web/src/features/sidebar/lib/status.ts` — status/indicator logic

For each file, read the full contents. Look for:
- Does `useWorkingSessionIds` actually subscribe to individual session statuses for ALL tab sessions, or just the active one?
- When the active tab changes, does the query for the previous session get unsubscribed?
- Is the unread store's `markUnread` actually being called?
- How does the tab render the unread dot — does it read from the unread store correctly?
- Any race conditions or missing dependencies in the useEffect?

Report back the exact data flow and where it breaks.

1→/**
     2→ * Sidebar Status Configuration System
     3→ *
     4→ * Centralized configuration for workspace/session statuses, priorities, and visual styling.
     5→ * Design philosophy: Make it easy to iterate on colors and priorities without touching component code.
     6→ *
     7→ * @see src/features/session/types.ts for SessionStatus type definition
     8→ */
     9→
    10→import type { Workspace } from "@/features/workspace/types";
    11→import type { WorkspaceStatus } from "@shared/enums";
    12→
    13→/**
    14→ * Display status extends SessionStatus with derived states.
    15→ * 'unread' is derived from needs_response / needs_plan_response session status.
    16→ */
    17→export type DisplayStatus = "idle" | "working" | "error" | "unread";
    18→
    19→/**
    20→ * Priority levels for sorting (higher = more urgent)
    21→ */
    22→export enum StatusPriority {
    23→  ERROR = 4, // Critical - something broke
    24→  UNREAD = 3, // Important - needs review / response
    25→  WORKING = 2, // Active - in progress
    26→  IDLE = 0, // Dormant - no activity
    27→}
    28→
    29→/**
    30→ * Visual configuration for each status
    31→ *
    32→ * Design System:
    33→ * - badge: Color for notification badges (collapsed sidebar)
    34→ * - border: Border/glow color for active states
    35→ * - text: Text color for status labels
    36→ * - bg: Background color for sections/cards
    37→ *
    38→ * Color Semantics:
    39→ * 🔴 Red (error) = Critical, blocking, needs immediate action
    40→ * 🟡 Amber (unread) = Important, needs attention when ready
    41→ * 🟢 Green (working) = Active, positive, making progress
    42→ * 🟣 Purple (compacting) = Transient, maintenance, ignore
    43→ * ⚪ Gray (idle) = Dormant, background, low priority
    44→ */
    45→export interface StatusConfig {
    46→  priority: StatusPriority;
    47→  label: string;
    48→  labelActive: string; // Present continuous form (e.g., "Working...")
    49→  badge: string; // Tailwind classes for badge
    50→  border: string; // Tailwind classes for border/glow
    51→  text: string; // Tailwind classes for text
    52→  bg: string; // Tailwind classes for background
    53→  pulse?: boolean; // Whether to animate/pulse
    54→}
    55→
    56→/**
    57→ * Status configuration map
    58→ *
    59→ * IMPORTANT: To change colors, edit this object (no component changes needed)
    60→ */
    61→export const STATUS_CONFIG: Record<DisplayStatus, StatusConfig> = {
    62→  error: {
    63→    priority: StatusPriority.ERROR,
    64→    label: "Error",
    65→    labelActive: "Error",
    66→    badge: "bg-destructive text-destructive-foreground",
    67→    border: "border-destructive/60",
    68→    text: "text-destructive",
    69→    bg: "bg-destructive/10",
    70→    pulse: true,
    71→  },
    72→  unread: {
    73→    priority: StatusPriority.UNREAD,
    74→    label: "Needs Attention",
    75→    labelActive: "Awaiting Input",
    76→    badge: "bg-status-unread text-status-unread-fg",
    77→    border: "border-status-unread/60",
    78→    text: "text-status-unread",
    79→    bg: "bg-status-unread/10",
    80→    pulse: false,
    81→  },
    82→  working: {
    83→    priority: StatusPriority.WORKING,
    84→    label: "Working",
    85→    labelActive: "Working...",
    86→    badge: "bg-primary text-primary-foreground",
    87→    border: "border-primary/60",
    88→    text: "text-primary",
    89→    bg: "bg-primary/10",
    90→    pulse: true,
    91→  },
    92→  idle: {
    93→    priority: StatusPriority.IDLE,
    94→    label: "Idle",
    95→    labelActive: "Idle",
    96→    badge: "bg-muted text-muted-foreground",
    97→    border: "border-muted",
    98→    text: "text-muted-foreground",
    99→    bg: "bg-muted/30",
   100→    pulse: false,
   101→  },
   102→};
   103→
   104→// ── Workflow Status (Linear-style) ────────────────────────────────────────
   105→// Separate from DisplayStatus which shows real-time agent activity.
   106→// Rendered as a small icon to the left of the workspace title.
   107→
   108→export interface WorkflowStatusConfig {
   109→  label: string;
   110→  color: string;
   111→}
   112→
   113→export const WORKFLOW_STATUS_CONFIG: Record<WorkspaceStatus, WorkflowStatusConfig> = {
   114→  backlog: { label: "Backlog", color: "text-muted-foreground" },
   115→  "in-progress": { label: "In Progress", color: "text-status-in-progress" },
   116→  "in-review": { label: "In Review", color: "text-status-in-review" },
   117→  done: { label: "Done", color: "text-status-done" },
   118→  canceled: { label: "Canceled", color: "text-muted-foreground" },
   119→};
   120→
   121→/**
   122→ * Derive display status from workspace data
   123→ *
   124→ * Priority logic:
   125→ * 1. Error — session in error state
   126→ * 2. Unread — agent needs user response OR has unseen activity
   127→ * 3. Working — agent actively processing
   128→ * 4. Idle — dormant
   129→ *
   130→ * @param workspace Workspace data with session info
   131→ * @param hasUnseenActivity Whether the session has activity the user hasn't viewed yet
   132→ * @returns Derived display status for UI rendering
   133→ */
   134→export function getDisplayStatus(workspace: Workspace, hasUnseenActivity = false): DisplayStatus {
   135→  const status = workspace.session_status;
   136→
   137→  if (status === "error") return "error";
   138→  if (status === "needs_response" || status === "needs_plan_response") return "unread";
   139→  if (status === "working") return "working";
   140→  if (hasUnseenActivity) return "unread";
   141→  return "idle";
   142→}
   143→
   144→/**
   145→ * Sort workspaces by status priority
   146→ * Higher priority statuses appear first
   147→ *
   148→ * @param workspaces Array of workspaces to sort
   149→ * @param unreadSessionIds Optional set of session IDs with unseen activity
   150→ * @returns Sorted array (original array not mutated)
   151→ */
   152→export function sortByStatusPriority<T extends Workspace>(
   153→  workspaces: T[],
   154→  unreadSessionIds?: Set<string>
   155→): T[] {
   156→  return [...workspaces].sort((a, b) => {
   157→    const hasUnreadA = !!(a.current_session_id && unreadSessionIds?.has(a.current_session_id));
   158→    const hasUnreadB = !!(b.current_session_id && unreadSessionIds?.has(b.current_session_id));
   159→    const statusA = getDisplayStatus(a, hasUnreadA);
   160→    const statusB = getDisplayStatus(b, hasUnreadB);
   161→    const priorityA = STATUS_CONFIG[statusA].priority;
   162→    const priorityB = STATUS_CONFIG[statusB].priority;
   163→
   164→    // Higher priority first
   165→    if (priorityA !== priorityB) {
   166→      return priorityB - priorityA;
   167→    }
   168→
   169→    // Same priority: sort by latest activity (most recent first)
   170→    const timeA = new Date(a.updated_at).getTime();
   171→    const timeB = new Date(b.updated_at).getTime();
   172→    return timeB - timeA;
   173→  });
   174→}
   175→
   176→

   271→            },
   272→            false,
   273→            "workspaceLayout/clearWorkspaceLayout"
   274→          ),
   275→
   276→        resetAll: () => set({ layouts: {} }, false, "workspaceLayout/resetAll"),
   277→      }),
   278→      {
   279→        name: "workspace-layout-store",
   280→        version: 10,
   281→        partialize: (state) => ({
   282→          ...state,
   283→          layouts: Object.fromEntries(
   284→            Object.entries(state.layouts).map(([id, layout]) => [
   285→              id,
   286→              {
   287→                ...layout,
   288→                pendingTerminalCommand: null,
   289→                terminalTabs: [],
   290→                activeTerminalTabId: null,
   291→                nextTerminalNum: 1,
   292→              },
   293→            ])
   294→          ),
   295→        }),
   296→        // No migration — stale v9 data falls back to defaultLayout
   297→        migrate: () => ({ layouts: {} }) as unknown as WorkspaceLayoutStore,
   298→      }
   299→    ),
   300→    {
   301→      name: "workspace-layout-store",
   302→      enabled: import.meta.env.DEV,
   303→    }
   304→  )
   305→);
   306→
   307→/**
   308→ * Stable Actions - Call from anywhere without causing re-renders
   309→ */
   310→export const workspaceLayoutActions = {
   311→  getLayout: (workspaceId: string) => useWorkspaceLayoutStore.getState().getLayout(workspaceId),
   312→  setLayout: (workspaceId: string, layout: Partial<WorkspaceLayoutState>) =>
   313→    useWorkspaceLayoutStore.getState().setLayout(workspaceId, layout),
   314→  setActiveContentTab: (workspaceId: string, tab: ContentTab) =>
   315→    useWorkspaceLayoutStore.getState().setActiveContentTab(workspaceId, tab),
   316→  setSelectedFilePath: (workspaceId: string, path: string | null) =>
   317→    useWorkspaceLayoutStore.getState().setSelectedFilePath(workspaceId, path),
   318→  setSidebarCollapsed: (workspaceId: string, collapsed: boolean) =>
   319→    useWorkspaceLayoutStore.getState().setSidebarCollapsed(workspaceId, collapsed),
   320→  setChatPanelCollapsed: (workspaceId: string, collapsed: boolean) =>
   321→    useWorkspaceLayoutStore.getState().setChatPanelCollapsed(workspaceId, collapsed),
   322→  setContentPanelCollapsed: (workspaceId: string, collapsed: boolean) =>
   323→    useWorkspaceLayoutStore.getState().setContentPanelCollapsed(workspaceId, collapsed),
   324→  setChatTabState: (workspaceId: string, sessionIds: string[], activeSessionId: string | null) =>
   325→    useWorkspaceLayoutStore.getState().setChatTabState(workspaceId, sessionIds, activeSessionId),
   326→  setTerminalTabState: (
   327→    workspaceId: string,
   328→    tabs: PersistedTerminalTab[],
   329→    activeTabId: string | null,
   330→    nextNum: number
   331→  ) =>
   332→    useWorkspaceLayoutStore.getState().setTerminalTabState(workspaceId, tabs, activeTabId, nextNum),
   333→  setPendingTerminalCommand: (workspaceId: string, command: string | null) =>
   334→    useWorkspaceLayoutStore.getState().setPendingTerminalCommand(workspaceId, command),
   335→  setSimulatorUdid: (workspaceId: string, udid: string | null) =>
   336→    useWorkspaceLayoutStore.getState().setSimulatorUdid(workspaceId, udid),
   337→  clearWorkspaceLayout: (workspaceId: string) =>
   338→    useWorkspaceLayoutStore.getState().clearWorkspaceLayout(workspaceId),
   339→  resetAll: () => useWorkspaceLayoutStore.getState().resetAll(),
   340→};
   341→

message.content: and so now if we hve teh ws we dont need the stale endpotn rght?

content.text: Right — with WS subscriptions handling freshness, the HTTP `queryFn` is just a fallback for initial load (before WS connects). The `staleTime: Infinity` I already set means it won't poll. The `queryFn` stays as the cold-start fallback but never refetches on its own. That's the correct setup — same pattern as `useSession` and `useMessages`.

diff --git a/.claude/agent-memory/code-reviewer/MEMORY.md b/.claude/agent-memory/code-reviewer/MEMORY.md
index d77257b5c..bbd9a9e11 100644
--- a/.claude/agent-memory/code-reviewer/MEMORY.md
+++ b/.claude/agent-memory/code-reviewer/MEMORY.md
@@ -297,6 +297,39 @@
   in `toolColors.ts`. Missing entries don't cause crashes but leave other components that derive color
   by tool name with `undefined` (falls through to no class).
 - When adding a new renderer, add its color entry to `TOOL_COLORS` in the same commit.
+## Unread Session Indicator Pattern (Confirmed)
+
+- Store: `useUnreadStore` in `features/session/store/unreadStore.ts` — Zustand + persist, `unreadSessionIds: Record<string, true>`.
+- Mark unread: `useGlobalSessionNotifications` fires on `working → idle|error|needs_response|needs_plan_response`
+  transition ONLY when `!isUserViewingSession(ws.id, sessionId)`.
+- Mark read: two sites — `handleWorkspaceClick` in MainLayout (sidebar click), and `handleTabChangeWithRead`
+  in ChatArea (tab switch).
+- **String-key serialization hack in RepositoryItem**: builds a comma-joined string of unread session IDs
+  from the store, then splits it back to a Set in useMemo. This is an anti-pattern. Fix: `useShallow` selector
+  that filters in the selector itself and returns a `Set<string>`. Saves ~18 lines.
+- **ChatArea full-map subscription**: `useUnreadStore((s) => s.unreadSessionIds)` subscribes to the entire
+  map — any workspace marking unread re-renders ChatArea. Fix: `useShallow` filtered to `chatSessionIds`.
+- **`Record<string, true>` vs `Set`**: Record is used because JSON doesn't natively serialize Set. The
+  tradeoff is real, but should be documented. Extract `isSessionUnread(s, id) => !!s.unreadSessionIds[id]`
+  helper so callers don't all inline `!!s.unreadSessionIds[id]`.
+- **`unreadActions` is redundant indirection**: every method delegates to `useUnreadStore.getState()`.
...
</persisted-output>

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix: unread session indicators for multi-tab workspaces

Add WebSocket subscriptions to useWorkingSessionIds so non-active tab
sessions receive real-time status updates. Previously only the active
tab had a WS subscription — background tabs relied on stale HTTP cache
and never triggered unread detection.

- Add WS subscribe() per tab session in useWorkingSessionIds
- Detect workspace switches to avoid cross-workspace false transitions
- Sidebar checks all tab sessions for unread, not just current_session_id
- Move unread dot from right side of tab to left, replacing the agent icon
- Only mark active tab as read on workspace click (preserve other tabs)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

content.text: There are many commits. The PR should focus on my commit. Let me create the PR:

input.command:
gh pr create --base main --title "fix: unread session indicators for multi-tab workspaces" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

- **Root cause:** `useWorkingSessionIds` had no WebSocket subscription — non-active tab sessions relied on stale HTTP cache (30s) and never received real-time status updates, so working → idle transitions were invisible
- **Fix:** Added `subscribe()` calls per tab session in `useWorkingSessionIds`, giving all tabs real-time WS push for session status changes
- **Sidebar:** `WorkspaceItem` now checks all tab session IDs for unread activity (via layout store), not just `current_session_id`
- **Tab UX:** Unread gold dot now replaces the agent icon on the left (instead of a separate dot on the right)
- **Read marking:** Clicking a workspace only marks the active tab's session as read — other tabs keep their unread dots until the user switches to them

## Test plan

- [ ] Open workspace, start agents on 2+ tabs, switch to a different tab — verify the background tab shows gold dot when its agent finishes
- [ ] Click a different workspace and come back — verify unread dots persist on non-active tabs
- [ ] Click an unread tab — verify the gold dot disappears and the agent icon returns
- [ ] Verify sidebar workspace dot shows when ANY tab session has unread activity

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"
